name: Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular
name_original: Visita en la Alcazaba de Vélez-Málaga y pueblos de La Axarquía – circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Vélez-Málaga offers a diverse landscape, combining challenging mountainous terrain with scenic coastal routes. The region is characterized by the imposing mountains of the Axarquía, including Sierra del Almijara, Tejeda, and Alhama, which provide significant elevation gains. Routes often wind through lush valleys with tropical fruit groves and past picturesque white villages. The area also features coastal roads with views of the Mediterranean Sea, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Vélez-Málaga The most popular road cycling route is Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular, a 33.2 miles (53.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic Alcazaba and several white villages of the Axarquía region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Axarquía White Villages Loop from Torre del Mar, a difficult 39.8 miles (64.1 km) path. This route offers views of the coast from Torre del Mar and winds through several traditional white villages. Local road cyclists also love the Sayalonga Town Center – Frigiliana loop from Vélez-Málaga, a 48.0 miles (77.3 km) trail leading through the white villages of Sayalonga and Frigiliana, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Vélez-Málaga is defined by mountainous climbs, scenic coastal roads, and routes through traditional white villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Vélez-Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 7,800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vélez-Málaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Vélez-Málaga?
A: Vélez-Málaga offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 70 tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Vélez-Málaga?
A: The region provides a diverse landscape for road cycling. You can expect challenging mountainous terrain in the Axarquía, including the Sierra del Almijara, Tejeda, and Alhama ranges, offering significant elevation gains. Routes also wind through lush valleys with tropical fruit groves and along scenic coastal roads with views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Vélez-Málaga?
A: Yes, Vélez-Málaga offers routes suitable for various abilities. There are 15 easy road cycling tours available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery without overly demanding climbs.

Q: Can I explore traditional white villages on road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass through the charming 'white villages' (Pueblos Blancos) scattered throughout the foothills of the Almijaras. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930351076/visit-the-alcazaba-of-velez-malaga-and-villages-of-la-axarquia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular&lt;/a&gt; route explores several of these historic hamlets. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932540417/axarquia-white-villages-loop-from-torre-del-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía White Villages Loop from Torre del Mar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the coast and winds through traditional villages.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover on road cycling routes in Vélez-Málaga?
A: The region is rich with stunning viewpoints. You might encounter vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/711229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Almijara&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the mountains. Other notable spots include the Ibn Batuta Viewpoint and Mirador Vélez-Málaga, providing spectacular vistas of the surrounding landscapes, coast, and historic towns.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the routes?
A: Yes, you can combine your ride with cultural exploration. The historic Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga, a 13th-century fortress, stands atop a hill with expansive views. The Church of Santa María la Mayor, with its Moorish and Gothic features, also offers beautiful city views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930351076/visit-the-alcazaba-of-velez-malaga-and-villages-of-la-axarquia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular&lt;/a&gt; route specifically includes the Alcazaba.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in the region?
A: Road cycling routes often feature impressive natural elements. You'll ride through areas with tropical fruit groves, see the scenic Lake Viñuela, and follow roads winding through valleys like the Rio Guaro. The coastal routes offer views of the Mediterranean Sea, and you might even glimpse the dramatic Barranco de Maro gorge.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Vélez-Málaga offers several long and difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolina Pass – Viewpoint Axarquia loop from La Viña&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 70.3-mile (113.1 km) route with over 2,000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39543431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Mar Lighthouse – Direct Ascent to Canillas de Aceituno loop from Busdiensten&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 58.4 miles (94 km).

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Vélez-Málaga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountainous climbs to scenic coastal roads and routes through traditional white villages. The variety ensures there's something for every preference.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Vélez-Málaga?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Vélez-Málaga are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930351076/visit-the-alcazaba-of-velez-malaga-and-villages-of-la-axarquia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit the Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga and villages of La Axarquía – circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932540417/axarquia-white-villages-loop-from-torre-del-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía White Villages Loop from Torre del Mar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Vélez-Málaga?
A: Vélez-Málaga, located in the Axarquía region of Andalusia, generally offers favorable conditions for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, ideal for tackling both coastal and mountain routes. Even winter can be suitable, with milder temperatures compared to many other European regions, making it a popular destination for cycling holidays.
tips:
The original lighthouse was built in 1864, but due to coastal erosion and other problems, it was rebuilt and relocated several times. The current lighthouse was finally built in its current position in 1976 after several moves.

The Torre del Mar lighthouse is not only a functional part of coastal navigation, but also a popular destination for a tour, especially at sunset. The lighthouse is a fine example of the combination of modern technology with the traditions of seafaring along the Spanish coast.

The ascent to the Alcazaba is especially worthwhile because of the great view of the city. Apart from the tower and remains of the outer walls, there is not much to marvel at in the ruins of the Fortaleza.
One of the white villages of Andalusia, in the Axarquía region. It has 3k inhabitants. The name comes from Arabic and means "son of Mocarra". In the architecture we will also find Arab features.
A 10 km stretch between El Borge and Benamargosa passing through Cútar, a very popular route among cyclists in the area. Great views of the white villages of the Axarquía region.
A small town of Arab origin, surrounded by fruit orchards: lemons, mangoes, avocados.

waypoints.name: Climb From El Borge to Cútar and Descent to Benamargosa
